label: Data
description: Are simply a value are set of values of different type which is called data types like string, integer, char etc. or collection of information.

label: Structure (Concept)
description: Way of organizing information, so that it is easier to use.

label: Data Objects
description: A unique location in computer memory that contains the values.

label: Algorithm
description: Step by Step procedure to solve a particular function.

label: Program
description: Algorithm + Data Structure.

label: Data Structure
description: A particular way of organizing data in a computer so that it can be used efficiently.

label: Efficiency
description: Allows data to be used efficiently.

label: Element Relationship
description: Consideration of elements stored and their relationship to each other.

label: Data Manipulation
description: A scheme for organizing related pieces of information that can be manipulated.

label: Why Data Structure
description: Necessity to solve complex requirements in an efficient way.

label: Complex Human Requirements
description: Human requirement with computer are going to complex day by day.

label: Fastest Solutions
description: Provides fastest solution of human requirements.

label: Efficient Solutions for Complex Problems
description: Provides efficient solution of complex problem.

label: Space Complexity Solution
description: Provides solution for Space Complexity.

label: Time Complexity Solution
description: Provides solution for Time Complexity.

label: Logical/Mathematical Description
description: Logical or mathematical description of the structure.

label: Structure Implementation
description: Implementation of structure on a computer using different computer languages.

label: C++ Language
description: A computer language mentioned for implementation purpose in the syllabus.

label: Quantitative Analysis
description: Determining the amount of memory needed to store the structure and the time required to process the structure.

label: Data Structure Classification
description: Categorization of data structures into types.

label: Primitive Data Structure
description: Data structures that are directly operated upon the machine-level instructions.

label: Built-in/Predefined Data Types
description: Standard / Predefine/ Built data types in of any one of the computer languages.

label: Non-Primitive Data Structure
description: Data structures that are derived from the primitive data structures.

label: User-Designed
description: Can be designed by user.

label: Homogeneous/Heterogeneous Grouping
description: Structuring of a group of homogeneous (same type) or heterogeneous (different type) data items.

label: Linear Data Structure
description: A data structure that has homogeneous elements and where elements are in a sequence.

label: Elements in a Sequence
description: Elements are in a sequence and form a liner series.

label: Non-Linear Data Structure
description: A data structure in which data item is connected to several other data items.

label: Hierarchical/Parent-Child Relationship
description: May exhibit either a hierarchical relationship or parent child relationship.

label: Static Data Structure
description: The size of the structure is fixed.

label: Dynamic Data Structure
description: The size of the structure in not fixed and can be modified during the operations performed on it.

label: Data Structure Operations
description: Commonly used operations performed on data structure.

label: Traversing
description: Processing each element in the list.

label: Search
description: Finding the location of the element with a given value or the Selection record with a given key.

label: Insertion
description: Adding a new element to the list.

label: Deletion
description: Removing an elements from the list.

label: Sorting
description: Arranging the elements in some type of order.

label: Merging
description: Combining two list into a single list.

label: Array
description: A non-primitive data structure that includes single dimensional and multi-dimensional types, and is an example of static and linear data structures.

label: Linked List
description: A non-primitive data structure, an example of dynamic and linear data structures.

label: Stack
description: An example of a linear data structure.

label: Queue
description: An example of a linear data structure.

label: Tree
description: An example of a non-linear data structure.

label: Graph
description: An example of a non-linear data structure.

label: Hash Table
description: An example of a non-linear data structure.

label: Integer
description: A type of primitive data structure.

label: Character
description: A type of primitive data structure.

label: Float
description: A type of primitive data structure.

label: Pointers
description: A type of primitive data structure.

label: Data Structures (Subject)
description: a new subject that is data structures.

label: Data (Core Concept)
description: a prerequisite before understanding what data structure is.

label: Data (Dictionary Definition)
description: quantities, characters or symbols on which operations are performed by a computer, which may be stored and transmitted in the form of electrical signals and recorded on magnetic, optical or mechanical recording media.

label: Operations on Data
description: operations performed by a computer on quantities, characters or symbols.

label: Storage and Transmission of Data
description: data may be stored and transmitted in the form of electrical signals and recorded on magnetic, optical or mechanical recording media.

label: Information
description: meaningful or processed data.

label: Data vs. Information Distinction
description: a distinction between data and an information, which is a very confusing topic.

label: Unprocessed Data (Example)
description: just a collection of characters or symbols where one won't be able to understand it properly.

label: Processed Data
description: data that has been manipulated, such as reversing a whole string, to make it understandable.

label: Meaningful Information (Goal)
description: information from which some meaning can be extracted, making it quite understandable.

label: Systematic Data Arrangement
description: arranging data in a systematic way so that it gets a structure and becomes meaningful.

label: Data Management
description: managing data in such a way so that it can produce some meaningful information.

label: Data Structure (Definition)
description: the systematic way to organize data so that it can be used efficiently.

label: Efficiency (Time/Space)
description: using data efficiently in terms of time as well as space.

label: Arrays (Example Data Structure)
description: a classical data structure, thought of as a sequence or collection of some symbols, characters, integers, or floats.

label: Stack Data Structure
description: a data structure used in implementing the redo and undo feature.

label: Undo/Redo Feature
description: a famous feature in applications like Google Docs, PowerPoint, or Microsoft Word that uses stack data structures.

label: Undo Stack
description: a stack where each information entered gets stored; elements are popped out when control z (undo) is pressed.

label: Redo Stack
description: a stack where elements popped from the undo stack are placed; used when control y (redo) is pressed to get the operation again.

label: Bitmap Images
description: images stored as a series of tiny dots called pixels.

label: Pixels
description: tiny dots or small squares, each assigned a color and then arranged in a pattern to form an image.

label: Two-Dimensional Array
description: a data structure (e.g., 37x40) used to store pixel information (each pixel's color) for bitmap images.

label: Graph Data Structure
description: a data structure used to store friendship information on social networking sites.

label: Graph Nodes
description: individuals (e.g., James, Mark, Leah, Lucy) represented in a graph data structure.

label: Graph Edges
description: connections or relationships between individuals (nodes) represented in a graph data structure.

label: Data Structures Importance
description: the reason why companies are preferring this subject over other subjects, as it is very important and useful in day-to-day life and used more frequently.
